title: Getting it
excerpt: The moment GenAI clicked — not as a tool but as something qualitatively different.

title: Innovative Laggard
excerpt: Being late to GenAI relative to your Twitter feed — but early relative to the world.

title: Becoming Unbounded
excerpt: When AI removes the skill bottleneck, you're no longer limited by what you can do — only by what you can imagine.

title: Pixel Art Dreams
excerpt: Midjourney started bleeding into my dreams. The model shapes how you see before you're even awake.

title: The Merge
excerpt: Failed projects combining into a natural save point. When the graveyard becomes the foundation.

title: Bricoleur
excerpt: Scraps of failed projects becoming successful ones. Making do with what's at hand.

title: History of Creativity
excerpt: Midjourney already contains the creative lineage I wanted to trace. The whole loop is already inside.

title: Seeing like a Prompter
excerpt: I was starting to see the world as potential prompts.

title: Oneshotting
excerpt: The calibration reflex — just try the hard thing first. The cost of asking has collapsed.

title: Someday/Maybe
excerpt: Dead ideas resurrected by AI. Someday/Maybe has arrived.

title: Breadcrumbs
excerpt: Borges as proxy-executor of ideas I couldn't yet build. Now it's easy to simulate worlds.

title: Blue Light
excerpt: Dreams to Midjourney to game concept to Claude mockup. The creative chain that runs through sleep.

title: A 4000 Year Run
excerpt: The canon is now finite. Everything ever written is inside the model. So I started at the beginning.

title: We Are All PMs Now
excerpt: The artist/friends/you-with-Claude reversal. I guess we are all PMs now.

title: Emoji Sigils
excerpt: Emojis as a prompt language. The grid is the argument.

title: Reading Simulator 9000
excerpt: A fictional device for the opportunity cost of finishing bad books.

title: MVC
excerpt: Borges quote. Three links. For sale: baby shoes, never worn.

title: Cambrian Explosion
excerpt: The internet was the Wild West. Then platforms flattened everything. AI is lowering the cost of making again.

title: Single-player Mode
excerpt: A solo operator with AI can now do what previously required a small company. Pieter Levels was just early.

title: Cheap Weird Things
excerpt: Low capital costs let you make the weird thing. Anime proved it. AI is the next step on that curve.

title: Bespoke Software
excerpt: Why use software built for the modal user when you can build exactly what you need?

title: Stewie Was Wrong
excerpt: What is a Midjourney prompt if not 'a pinch of this, a pinch of that'?

title: The Prompter Is the Prompted
excerpt: Are you prompting it for answers? Or is it prompting you for questions?

title: Dying by Inches
excerpt: Darwin was dying by inches from not having anyone to talk to about insects. That is exactly how I felt about GenAI.

title: Imaginal Amputees
excerpt: For the first time in human history our imagination is not the bottleneck anymore.

title: Midjourney Gospel
excerpt: Ten commandments for working with generative AI. Thou shalt give grace.

title: Original Sin
excerpt: Anthropic accused labs of stealing. The internet sided with the thieves. Illegal — but not immoral?

title: Scriptorium
excerpt: Opened Aristotle's Metaphysics by accident. And then it hit me — why not?

title: Is It Still There?
excerpt: Every year the digital texts grow lighter and clearer. Every year the paper books grow heavier with reality.

title: Inverse Science
excerpt: Classical science: observe, theorize, test, build. AI inverts this. Build, test, discover — then what?
